text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-off after Iran escalation, small caps rallied anyway, energy cratered, vol spiked selective. Markets bid small/value despite S&P 500 -0.20%, QQQ -0.32%—geopolitical fear priced unevenly across cap, with commodities and housing outperforming tech. Ceasefire hopes from last week gave way to renewed tensions Monday as US seized Iran-flagged ship, triggering oil -7.79% but permitting equity rotation into cyclicals and metals.

INDICES — VIX 18.9, down from 23.0 MA20, signals contained fear despite headline risk. Small caps led: IWM +2.16%, IJR +2.03%, IJH +1.97% vs large cap weakness: SPY -0.20%, QQQ -0.32%, DIA +0.02%. Small > large by 2.36pp. Most notable cross-asset divergence: oil USO -7.79% alongside gold GLD +1.33% and silver SLV +3.35%—classic risk-off commodity split.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.5x (below avg 0.68x), QQQ also 0.5x, indicating low institutional conviction on downside. IWM 1.1x, SLV 0.9x gaining attention. Interpretation: vol down + price down = capitulation-lite, not panic. XLE 1.6x accumulating despite -2.76% close—dealer/macro hedges rebalancing into energy weakness.

SECTORS — XLY Discretionary +2.36%, XLI Industrials +1.87%, XLK Technology +1.53%, XLRE Real Estate +1.53%, XLV Healthcare +1.49%, XLP Staples +1.26%, XLF Financials +0.77%, XLB Materials +0.25%, XLC Comms +0.23%, XLU Utilities -0.41%, XLE Energy -2.76%. Top: XLY, XLI. Bottom: XLE, XLU. Energy reversing last week's gains as oil tanked on Hormuz supply fears reversed by US military action.

THE 7s — Housing 7 +3.50% (WGMI +5.35% strength, no weak ticker flagged), Old Metals 7 +2.84% (SLV vol spike, GLD holding), Mag 7 +1.66%, Freight 7 +1.50%, Buildout 7 +1.11%. Bottom: Energy 7 -2.80%, Power 7 -0.95%, New Metals 7 -0.91%, Fab 7 -0.72%, Defense 7 -0.30%. Energy 7 shows divergence—XLE -2.76% but XOP -4.84%, energy producers hit harder than sector ETF.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC Apr 29. CME hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0%. Hawk tally 6 (Cook, Hammack, Kashkari, Logan, Barkin, Goolsbee), neutral 5, dove 4. USD liquidity stress 41/100 (Normal). SOFR dispersion 0.13% (23rd percentile, least stressed component). TED spread 0.09%, ON-RRP $0.5B—no stress signals. MOVE 65.7 below MA20 85.4, bond vol cooling despite CPI YoY 3.32% hawk tilt.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR 1.28 (elevated protection demand), skew +6.7% (put premium, fear bid). QQQ PCR 2.71 (extreme hedge demand), skew +7.3%, IV 20.6% above SPY 15.7%—small/growth positioning heavy defensive. IWM PCR 2.11, skew +6.0%, IV 24.8%. GEX flip SPY 715 vs spot ~7103—price near gamma flip zone; dealer hedging could amplify moves intraday. VIX 18.9 < MA20 23.0 = complacency erosion, not panic.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil -7.79% (USO) amid Hormuz tensions but gold +1.33%, silver +3.35%—risk-off commodity signal fracturing. Dollar bid (ceasefire hopes fading) pressuring gold slightly; silver outperforming on industrial demand and volatility skew (IV 57.3%). Energy weakness not transmitting to broad equities—cyclical/small rotation absorbing oil decline.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 60.4/100 (Greed, elevated complacency). Top 3 movers: NFLX -9.72% (New Staples, 3.2x vol—distribution on earnings miss), ALB -8.29% (New Metals, 2.0x vol—lithium weakness, distribution), USO -7.79% (cross-asset). 20d momentum: best sector XLK +11.64%, worst XLU -0.12%; best basket Semi 7 +32.46%, worst Software 7 -7.03%. NFLX vol spike = distribution; earnings headwind. Iran ship seizure tied oil selloff; ceasefire narrative cracking.

WATCH — Netflix NFLX -9.72%, earnings tail risk, monitor for reversal or further capitulation. Albemarle ALB -8.29%, lithium cycle break, watch support near $172 price target. USO -7.79%, gamma flip near $25—if breached lower, oil could cascade further; monitor geopolitical headlines for supply flow changes.
